title: rubus × semiplicatus f. nonradicans
intro: Rubus × semiplicatus f. nonradicans, a lesser-known variant of the Rubus semiplicatus species, is a hybrid of the Himalayan blackberry and a non-climbing form of the Himalayan blackberry. It has been found to possess medicinal properties, particularly in its antioxidant and anti-inflammatory capabilities. The plant has been traditionally used to treat various health issues, including digestive problems, fever, and skin conditions, offering potential health benefits such as improved gut health and reduced inflammation. The leaves and stems of Rubus × semiplicatus f. nonradicans can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or decoctions for medicinal purposes.
